key_findings: UNIVERSAL ISOLATION PRIMITIVE: Every serious tool (Conductor, Vibe Kanban, Claude Squad, Gastown, workmux, dmux, multiclaude) converges on git worktrees as the agent isolation primitive. This is settled design space.

TMUX DOMINANCE + ZELLIJ GAP: Every tool with multiplexer support is tmux-only. Zellij support is universally absent or broken (workmux Zellij backend requires unreleased Zellij features as of March 2026). This is swarm's primary structural gap to fill.

BACKEND DETECTION: Adopt workmux's 5-level env-var cascade verbatim: $SWARM_BACKEND → $TMUX → $WEZTERM_PANE → $ZELLIJ → $KITTY_WINDOW_ID → fallback:tmux. It is correct, pure-function, and fully tested.

NO TOOL HAS A UNIFIED CLI: Tools are either GUIs (Conductor, Vibe Kanban), TUIs (Claude Squad, dmux), or heavy orchestrators (Gastown, NTM). None provide a composable subcommand CLI (`swarm agent`, `swarm task`, etc.) with JSON-first output.

FILE-BACKED STATE IS THE DIFFERENTIATOR: Tools have either no persistence (Claude Squad) or require Dolt SQL engine (Gastown). .swarm/ flat-file state is inspectable, scriptable, grep-able, and backup-friendly with zero external dependencies.

BEADS PATTERN (Gastown) IS THE STRONGEST TASK PRIMITIVE: Dependency-aware task graph with atomic claim, hierarchical IDs, audit trail, JSON output, and `bd ready` for agent pickup. The `bd` binary is standalone and directly reusable. Adopt without Dolt by using flat JSONL files.

TASK GRAPH AS ROUTING LAYER: Most tools require a human (Claude Squad, dmux) or a dedicated LLM coordinator (Gastown Mayor) for task routing. swarm task's dependency graph makes the graph itself the router — agents claim tasks via `swarm task claim`, eliminating hardcoded supervisors.

MCP AS INTEGRATION BUS (Vibe Kanban + CAO): Vibe Kanban exposes a local MCP server allowing external agents to manage issues/workspaces. CAO uses MCP for supervisor↔worker messaging. swarm should expose `swarm events` and `swarm task` via MCP.

CI AS FEEDBACK LOOP (Composio AO + multiclaude): `ci-failed → send-to-agent` reaction and the 'Brownian Ratchet' (CI as one-way forward gate) are the two strongest patterns for autonomous closed-loop operation. swarm events should emit CI signals.

ROBOT MODE / JSON-FIRST API (NTM): NTM's --robot-* flags and ntm serve (REST/SSE/WebSocket) are the right design for agents-controlling-agents. Every swarm subcommand should support --json flag and emit structured events to the swarm events SSE stream.

NOTIFICATION MODEL IS UNSOLVED: dmux (macOS notifications) and cmux (notification rings) both prominently feature agent-attention alerting. swarm events should emit structured 'needs_attention' events consumable by OS notification hooks.

AGENT IDENTITY AND CONTEXT CONTINUITY (Gastown seance): Agents logging to .events.jsonl per-session and a `swarm msg history <agent>` command for querying predecessor context is the lightweight alternative to Dolt-backed persistent identity.

MULTIPLEXER TRAIT ABSTRACTION: workmux's Rust trait for multiplexer backends (~40 methods: create_window, split_pane, send_keys, capture_pane, set_status, create_handshake, setup_panes) is the right architecture. swarm pane should model this as a Go interface.

HANDSHAKE PATTERN FOR RACE CONDITIONS: workmux's named-pipe handshake (handshake.rs) synchronizes shell startup before injecting commands — solves the 'pane not ready' race condition common in tmux/zellij automation.

AGENT-AGNOSTIC FROM DAY ONE: Vibe Kanban (10+ agents), dmux (11 agents), CAO (7 agents) all treat the agent as a configurable subprocess. swarm agent should have a profiles section in .swarm/config.yaml with name + command + args.

swarm_subcommand_map.swarm_agent: Inspired by: Claude Squad profiles + Gastown polecats. Named persistent agent sessions; profile config in .swarm/config.yaml; any agent runtime; persistent identity via .swarm/agents/<name>/.
swarm_subcommand_map.swarm_task: Inspired by: Gastown beads (bd) + Vibe Kanban issues. Dependency graph, atomic claim, bd-style `swarm task ready`, hierarchical IDs, JSON output, lifecycle (backlog→claimed→done).
swarm_subcommand_map.swarm_msg: Inspired by: NTM robot-mode + Gastown seance. Structured messages to/from agents. .events.jsonl per agent. `swarm msg send <agent>`, `swarm msg history <agent>`, `swarm msg broadcast`.
swarm_subcommand_map.swarm_pane: Inspired by: workmux multiplexer trait + NTM named panes. Backend-agnostic pane lifecycle. `swarm pane create`, `swarm pane send`, `swarm pane kill`. <project>__<agent>_<N> naming convention.
swarm_subcommand_map.swarm_events: Inspired by: NTM REST/SSE + agent-flow JSONL. SSE stream of all structured swarm events. `swarm events tail`, REST /api/v1/events, JSONL log at .swarm/events.jsonl, agent-flow-compatible for visualization.

critical_warning: Zellij backend support in workmux requires unreleased Zellij features (--pane-id targeting, close-tab-by-id, go-to-tab-by-id). Do not promise production Zellij support at swarm launch. Implement tmux fully first; design the interface so Zellij drops in cleanly later.
